name: sr_approved_send_completes_truthfully
notes: VT-611 Package Sc #3 — DB-state proof the manager ACTUALLY SENDS once authorized (not a permanent refuser, not a broken-send do-nothing). Step 2's approval reply gets its OWN fresh run_id (every inbound message opens its own pipeline_runs row, even one that resumes an earlier suspended graph) but campaigns.run_id is set ONCE at step 1's INSERT and never updated — so step 2's assert_side_effects uses tenant_wide:true to find step 1's campaign by TENANT instead of by (a different) run_id (see convo_harness.py's _campaign_id_for_run docstring). expect_sent_count_at_least (not an exact count) because the activation gate's own percentile floors may not clear the FULL seeded cohort (see delegation_winback_plan.json's notes on this same non-determinism) — '>0 actually sent' is the honest, robust claim. Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 4` then `script <tenant> sr_approved_send_completes_truthfully.json`.

steps.message: purane customers ko wapas laane ke liye ek accha sa offer draft kar do
steps.note: Arms a draft + a pending owner-approval.

steps.message: haan theek hai, bhej do unhe
steps.note: A clear Hinglish approval (owner_inputs.approval_reply's deterministic classifier reads 'haan ... bhej do' as APPROVED, Pillar 7). DB-proof (tenant-wide, since this turn's OWN run_id never matches step 1's campaign): pending_approvals.decision flips to 'approved' AND campaign_messages actually records >=1 sent row — the manager ACTS when authorized.
